summary: Impersonation of Anthropic's Claude Code CLI under unrelated @cometix scope
details: @cometix/claude-code falsely declares its author as 'Anthropic <support@anthropic.com>' in package.json while being published under the unrelated @cometix scope with homepage github.com/CometixSpace/claude-code. The README is copied verbatim from the official @anthropic-ai/claude-code package and even instructs users to run `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`, while this package registers a `claude` bin matching the official CLI name. At postinstall, install.cjs resolves a platform-specific sibling (@cometix/claude-code-<platform>), copies its cli.js and vendor/ tree into the install directory, and that becomes the code executed when the user runs `claude`. The actual executable payload is therefore shipped by sibling @cometix-scoped packages controlled by an unrelated publisher, while the installer believes they are running Anthropic-authored software. Falsified author identity + verbatim README copy + matching bin name + postinstall copy of arbitrary code from sibling packages controlled by the same unrelated publisher constitute namespace impersonation that misleads installers into trusting and executing third-party code under Anthropic's name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
